title: Things to Do in Orlando
pageHeading: 50 Best Things to Do in Orlando FL
pageSubtitle: The ultimate guide to attractions, activities, dining, and experiences in Orlando, Florida.
introContent: Orlando is one of the most visited cities in the world, welcoming over 75 million visitors annually. Beyond the world-famous theme parks, Orlando offers a vibrant food scene, beautiful nature preserves, world-class shopping, and cultural attractions that surprise first-time visitors.

activities.name: Walt Disney World
activities.description: The world's most visited theme park destination featuring four major parks - Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, Hollywood Studios, and Animal Kingdom.

activities.name: Universal Studios Florida
activities.description: Movie-themed rides and attractions including Hagrid's Magical Creatures, Velocicoaster, and the Wizarding World of Harry Potter.

activities.name: SeaWorld Orlando
activities.description: Marine life park featuring roller coasters, animal encounters, and educational exhibits about ocean conservation.

activities.name: ICON Park
activities.description: Entertainment complex on International Drive featuring The Wheel, SEA LIFE Aquarium, and Madame Tussauds wax museum.

activities.name: Disney Springs
activities.description: Massive open-air shopping, dining, and entertainment complex at Walt Disney World with over 100 stores and restaurants.

activities.name: Harry P. Leu Gardens
activities.description: 50-acre botanical garden in downtown Orlando featuring tropical plants, butterfly gardens, and historic Leu House Museum.

activities.name: Gatorland
activities.description: The Alligator Capital of the World since 1949, featuring thousands of alligators, crocodiles, and the Screamin Gator Zip Line.

activities.name: Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts
activities.description: World-class performing arts center hosting Broadway shows, concerts, ballet, and comedy in downtown Orlando.

activities.name: Wekiwa Springs State Park
activities.description: Natural spring swimming area with crystal-clear 72-degree water, plus hiking trails, canoeing, and kayaking along the Wekiva River.

activities.name: Park Avenue Winter Park
activities.description: Charming tree-lined shopping district in Winter Park with boutiques, galleries, restaurants, and the Charles Hosmer Morse Museum of American Art.

activities.name: Kennedy Space Center
activities.description: NASA's launch headquarters on the Space Coast, about an hour east of Orlando, featuring rocket exhibits, astronaut encounters, and launch viewing.

activities.name: Topgolf Orlando
activities.description: High-tech driving range and entertainment venue with climate-controlled hitting bays, full restaurant, and rooftop terrace.

activities.name: Orlando Museum of Art
activities.description: Central Florida's premier art museum featuring American art, African art, and rotating exhibitions in Loch Haven Park.

activities.name: Blue Spring State Park
activities.description: One of Florida's best manatee viewing spots in winter months, with a crystal-clear spring run, swimming, and nature trails.

activities.name: Fun Spot America
activities.description: Family-friendly amusement park with go-karts, roller coasters, and rides on International Drive - free parking and free admission with pay-per-ride.

title: Things to Do in Tampa
pageHeading: 40 Best Things to Do in Tampa FL
pageSubtitle: Explore Tampa Bay's top attractions, restaurants, waterfront experiences, and cultural highlights.
introContent: Tampa is a vibrant Gulf Coast city known for its rich Cuban heritage, world-class beaches, craft beer scene, and the annual Gasparilla pirate festival. From the historic streets of Ybor City to the Tampa Riverwalk, the city blends culture, nature, and urban energy.

activities.name: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay
activities.description: Africa-themed theme park with world-class roller coasters including SheiKra, Cheetah Hunt, and Iron Gwazi, plus animal encounters.

activities.name: Tampa Riverwalk
activities.description: 2.6-mile waterfront walkway connecting downtown Tampa's top attractions including museums, parks, restaurants, and the Convention Center.

activities.name: Ybor City
activities.description: Tampa's historic Latin Quarter with cigar shops, nightlife, restaurants, and the Columbia Restaurant - Florida's oldest restaurant.

activities.name: The Florida Aquarium
activities.description: Top-rated aquarium on the Tampa waterfront featuring coral reef exhibits, penguin encounters, shark swim programs, and wild dolphin cruises.

activities.name: Raymond James Stadium
activities.description: Home of the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and host of multiple Super Bowls, featuring stadium tours and major concert events.

activities.name: Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino
activities.description: Guitar-shaped luxury resort with gaming, dining at Council Oak, live entertainment, and a massive pool complex.

activities.name: Bayshore Boulevard
activities.description: The world's longest continuous sidewalk at 4.5 miles, popular for jogging, biking, and sunset views along Hillsborough Bay.

activities.name: Tampa Museum of Art
activities.description: Contemporary and classical art museum on the Riverwalk with rotating exhibitions and a stunning waterfront building.

activities.name: ZooTampa at Lowry Park
activities.description: Top-rated zoo featuring Florida wildlife, African elephants, Indian rhinos, and the Roaring Springs waterpark ride.

activities.name: Clearwater Beach Day Trip
activities.description: America's top-rated beach is just 30 minutes from downtown Tampa, featuring white sand, Pier 60 sunset festival, and crystal-clear Gulf waters.

activities.name: Tampa Craft Beer Trail
activities.description: Tampa Bay is home to over 80 craft breweries including Cigar City Brewing, Coppertail Brewing, and Hidden Springs Ale Works.

activities.name: Amalie Arena
activities.description: Home of the Tampa Bay Lightning NHL team and a premier concert and event venue on the downtown waterfront.

title: Things to Do in Fort Lauderdale
pageHeading: 35 Best Things to Do in Fort Lauderdale FL
pageSubtitle: Your guide to Fort Lauderdale's beaches, waterways, dining, and arts scene.
introContent: Fort Lauderdale is known as the Venice of America for its 165 miles of navigable waterways. The city has transformed from a spring break party town into a sophisticated destination with world-class dining, arts, and a stunning beachfront promenade.

activities.name: Fort Lauderdale Beach
activities.description: Miles of pristine golden sand with a beautiful wave wall promenade, beachfront restaurants, and watersport rentals.

activities.name: Las Olas Boulevard
activities.description: Fort Lauderdale's premier shopping and dining street featuring boutiques, galleries, sidewalk cafes, and nightlife.

activities.name: Jungle Queen Riverboat
activities.description: Iconic sightseeing cruise along the New River and Intracoastal Waterway viewing millionaire mansions and tropical scenery since 1935.

activities.name: NSU Art Museum
activities.description: Major contemporary art museum on Las Olas designed by architect David Adjaye, featuring one of the largest collections of contemporary art in Florida.

activities.name: Bonnet House Museum & Gardens
activities.description: Historic estate and tropical gardens on the beach featuring art, nature trails, monkeys, and swan-filled waterways.

activities.name: Water Taxi Fort Lauderdale
activities.description: Hop-on, hop-off water taxi service connecting hotels, restaurants, and attractions along the Intracoastal and New River.

activities.name: Museum of Discovery and Science
activities.description: Interactive science museum with IMAX theater, otters, and the Great Gravity Clock - the largest kinetic energy sculpture in Florida.

activities.name: Deerfield Beach Fishing Pier
activities.description: Historic 920-foot fishing pier with bait shop, restaurant, and ocean views just north of Fort Lauderdale.

activities.name: Hugh Taylor Birch State Park
activities.description: 180-acre coastal park between the Intracoastal and Atlantic Ocean with kayaking, swimming, nature trails, and a freshwater lagoon.

activities.name: Broward Center for the Performing Arts
activities.description: Premier performing arts venue hosting Broadway tours, concerts, comedy, and cultural events on the New River.

title: Things to Do in Cocoa Beach
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do in Cocoa Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Explore Florida's Space Coast beaches, surfing, rocket launches, and waterfront dining.
introContent: Cocoa Beach is the heart of Florida's Space Coast, offering world-class surfing, stunning Atlantic beaches, and a front-row seat to rocket launches from Kennedy Space Center. The laid-back beach town vibe pairs perfectly with fresh seafood and tropical sunrises.

activities.name: Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex
activities.description: Experience NASA's launch headquarters with rocket exhibits, astronaut meet-and-greets, shuttle Atlantis exhibit, and bus tours to launch pads.

activities.name: Cocoa Beach Surfing
activities.description: The East Coast surfing capital with consistent waves, surf schools, and the legendary Ron Jon Surf Shop - the world's largest surf shop.

activities.name: Cocoa Beach Pier
activities.description: 800-foot fishing pier stretching into the Atlantic with restaurants, bars, live music, and great views for watching rocket launches.

activities.name: Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge
activities.description: 140,000-acre wildlife refuge adjacent to Kennedy Space Center featuring manatees, bald eagles, alligators, and the Black Point Wildlife Drive.

activities.name: Canaveral National Seashore
activities.description: 24 miles of undeveloped Atlantic beach - one of the longest stretches of natural shoreline on Florida's east coast.

activities.name: Ron Jon Surf Shop
activities.description: The world's largest surf shop at 52,000 square feet, open 24 hours, with everything from surfboards to Florida souvenirs.

activities.name: Rocket Launch Viewing
activities.description: Watch SpaceX, NASA, and ULA rockets launch from Cape Canaveral with viewing spots along the beach, Jetty Park, and Space View Park.

activities.name: Port Canaveral
activities.description: One of the world's busiest cruise ports with waterfront dining, fishing charters, and the Exploration Tower observation deck.

activities.name: Jetty Park Beach & Campground
activities.description: Popular beach and park at the mouth of Port Canaveral, ideal for watching cruise ships, fishing, and rocket launch viewing.

activities.name: Dolphin & Manatee Tours
activities.description: Kayak and boat tours through the Indian River Lagoon and Banana River to see dolphins, manatees, and sea turtles in their natural habitat.

title: Things to Do in Cape Canaveral
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Cape Canaveral FL
pageSubtitle: Discover rocket launches, cruise port dining, fishing, and Space Coast adventures in Cape Canaveral.
introContent: Cape Canaveral is where space exploration and coastal Florida meet. Home to the Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and Port Canaveral, the area offers rocket launch viewing, waterfront dining, fishing charters, and easy access to Kennedy Space Center.

activities.name: Cape Canaveral Space Force Station
activities.description: Active military installation where SpaceX, ULA, and other providers launch rockets regularly. Public viewing areas along the coast offer spectacular launch experiences.

activities.name: Exploration Tower
activities.description: Seven-story observation tower at Port Canaveral with exhibits about the space program, local nature, and 360-degree views of launches, cruise ships, and the coast.

activities.name: Port Canaveral Fishing Charters
activities.description: Deep-sea and inshore fishing charters depart from the port daily targeting mahi-mahi, wahoo, snapper, and sailfish in the Gulf Stream.

activities.name: Jetty Park Beach
activities.description: 4.5-acre beachfront park at the port entrance, ideal for swimming, fishing, camping, and watching cruise ships pass within yards of the shore.

activities.name: Port Canaveral Waterfront Dining
activities.description: The Cove at Port Canaveral features waterfront restaurants including Grills, Fishlips, and Rusty's with views of cruise ships and fishing boats.

activities.name: Dolphin Watching Cruises
activities.description: Eco-tour boats depart from Port Canaveral for dolphin sightings in the Banana River and Indian River Lagoon.

activities.name: Cape Canaveral Beach
activities.description: Quiet, uncrowded Atlantic beach popular with locals for surfing, sunbathing, and morning walks along the shore.

activities.name: Cruise Terminal Shopping
activities.description: Port Canaveral features duty-free shops and specialty stores for cruise passengers and visitors.

activities.name: Manatee Sanctuary Park
activities.description: Waterfront park with a playground, walking trails, and a canal where manatees congregate during cooler months.

activities.name: Duran Golf Club
activities.description: Championship 18-hole golf course in nearby Viera with Audubon-certified environmental stewardship and modern facilities.

title: Things to Do in St. Petersburg
pageHeading: 35 Best Things to Do in St. Petersburg FL
pageSubtitle: Art, beaches, craft beer, and waterfront culture in the Sunshine City.
introContent: St. Petersburg has transformed into one of Florida's most vibrant cultural destinations, with world-class museums, a thriving street art scene, award-winning restaurants, and the beautiful downtown waterfront. The city holds a Guinness World Record for 768 consecutive days of sunshine.

activities.name: The Dali Museum
activities.description: Houses the world's most comprehensive collection of Salvador Dali's works, including 8 masterpiece paintings, in a stunning geodesic glass building.

activities.name: St. Pete Murals & Street Art
activities.description: Over 300 murals transform downtown St. Pete into an open-air gallery, with the annual SHINE Mural Festival adding new works each year.

activities.name: St. Pete Beach
activities.description: Consistently ranked among America's best beaches with sugar-white sand, calm Gulf waters, and the iconic Don CeSar pink palace hotel.

activities.name: St. Pete Craft Beer Trail
activities.description: Over 40 craft breweries in the St. Pete area including Green Bench, Cycle Brewing, and 3 Daughters Brewing - one of Florida's top beer cities.

activities.name: The St. Pete Pier
activities.description: Iconic 26-acre waterfront destination with restaurants, a splash pad, art installations, fishing, and panoramic Tampa Bay views.

activities.name: Museum of Fine Arts
activities.description: Comprehensive art museum on Beach Drive featuring collections from ancient to contemporary, including Monet, O'Keeffe, and Rodin.

activities.name: Beach Drive
activities.description: Downtown St. Pete's premier waterfront street lined with upscale restaurants, boutiques, galleries, and sidewalk cafes.

activities.name: Sunken Gardens
activities.description: A 100-year-old botanical garden featuring over 50,000 tropical plants, flamingos, and a butterfly garden in a lush sinkhole setting.

activities.name: Fort De Soto Park
activities.description: 1,136-acre park on five interconnected islands with pristine beaches, kayak trails, a historic fort, and some of Florida's best shelling.

activities.name: The Mahaffey Theater
activities.description: Waterfront performing arts venue hosting concerts, Broadway shows, and the Florida Orchestra on Tampa Bay.

title: Things to Do in Clearwater
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do in Clearwater FL
pageSubtitle: America's best beach plus marine life, sunset festivals, and Gulf Coast adventures.
introContent: Clearwater is home to one of America's highest-rated beaches with powdery white sand and calm, turquoise Gulf waters. Beyond the beach, the city offers marine life encounters, waterfront dining, and the famous Pier 60 sunset celebration every evening.

activities.name: Clearwater Beach
activities.description: Consistently voted the best beach in America by TripAdvisor, featuring sugar-white sand, calm Gulf waters, and a lively beachfront strip.

activities.name: Clearwater Marine Aquarium
activities.description: Home of Winter the dolphin from Dolphin Tale, this rescue aquarium rehabilitates sea turtles, dolphins, and other marine animals.

activities.name: Pier 60 Sunset Festival
activities.description: Nightly sunset celebration at Pier 60 with street performers, artisan vendors, and spectacular Gulf of Mexico sunsets.

activities.name: Clearwater Dolphin Cruises
activities.description: Boat tours departing from Clearwater Beach Marina to see bottlenose dolphins, sea birds, and the Gulf coastline.

activities.name: Caladesi Island State Park
activities.description: Accessible only by boat or ferry, this pristine barrier island features untouched beaches, kayak trails through mangroves, and nature walks.

activities.name: Frenchy's on the Beach
activities.description: Iconic Clearwater Beach restaurant chain famous for its super grouper sandwich and fresh Gulf seafood with toes-in-the-sand dining.

activities.name: Deep Sea Fishing Charters
activities.description: Gulf of Mexico fishing trips from Clearwater Beach targeting grouper, snapper, king mackerel, and tarpon.

activities.name: Sand Key Park
activities.description: 65-acre county park with beautiful beaches, nature trails, a dog park, and fewer crowds than Clearwater Beach proper.

activities.name: Clearwater Beach Walk
activities.description: Beachfront promenade connecting hotels, restaurants, and shops along the Gulf with public art installations and water features.

activities.name: Capitol Theatre
activities.description: Beautifully restored 1921 theater in downtown Clearwater hosting concerts, plays, comedy shows, and film screenings.

title: Things to Do in Daytona Beach
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do in Daytona Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Racing, beaches, boardwalk fun, and motorsports heritage in the World's Most Famous Beach.
introContent: Daytona Beach is the birthplace of NASCAR and home to the Daytona International Speedway. The wide, hard-packed beach allows cars to drive on the sand, a tradition dating back to early land speed record attempts. Beyond racing, the city offers a classic boardwalk, excellent surfing, and a growing arts scene.

activities.name: Daytona International Speedway
activities.description: Home of the Daytona 500, this iconic 2.5-mile superspeedway offers behind-the-scenes tours, the Motorsports Hall of Fame, and thrilling race experiences.

activities.name: Daytona Beach
activities.description: 23 miles of white-sand beach where cars are still permitted to drive on designated sections - a Daytona tradition since the early 1900s.

activities.name: Daytona Beach Boardwalk
activities.description: Classic oceanfront boardwalk with amusement rides, arcades, the Bandshell amphitheater, and the historic Main Street Pier.

activities.name: Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse
activities.description: Florida's tallest lighthouse at 175 feet, built in 1887, with panoramic views from the top and a maritime museum.

activities.name: Richard Petty Driving Experience
activities.description: Drive or ride in a real NASCAR stock car at Daytona International Speedway at speeds up to 160 mph.

activities.name: Marine Science Center
activities.description: Rehabilitation center for sea turtles and seabirds with nature trails, a bird sanctuary, and hands-on exhibits in Ponce Inlet.

activities.name: Daytona Beach Bandshell
activities.description: Historic oceanfront amphitheater hosting free concerts, festivals, and community events on the beach boardwalk.

activities.name: New Smyrna Beach Surfing
activities.description: Just south of Daytona, New Smyrna Beach is one of the best surfing spots on the East Coast with consistent breaks.

activities.name: Tanger Outlets Daytona Beach
activities.description: Open-air outlet shopping center with over 80 brand-name stores near the Speedway.

activities.name: Tomoka State Park
activities.description: Nature park at the confluence of the Tomoka and Halifax Rivers with kayaking, fishing, camping, and hiking through hammock forests.

title: Things to Do in Kissimmee
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Kissimmee FL
pageSubtitle: Family fun, nature, and easy access to Orlando's theme parks from Kissimmee.
introContent: Kissimmee sits at the gateway to Walt Disney World and offers a mix of Florida nature, family attractions, and budget-friendly accommodations. The area features airboat rides, Old Town entertainment, and beautiful lakefront scenery along Lake Toho.

activities.name: Wild Florida Airboats
activities.description: Airboat tours through Central Florida wetlands spotting alligators, birds, and native wildlife, plus a drive-through safari park.

activities.name: Old Town Kissimmee
activities.description: Nostalgic entertainment district with shops, restaurants, go-karts, and Saturday night classic car cruises.

activities.name: Lake Tohopekaliga Fishing
activities.description: One of Florida's premier bass fishing lakes with trophy largemouth bass, professional guides, and scenic lakefront parks.

activities.name: Medieval Times
activities.description: Dinner theater featuring jousting knights, falconry, and a four-course medieval feast in a castle setting.

activities.name: Fun Spot America Kissimmee
activities.description: Family amusement park with roller coasters, go-karts, and rides with free admission and pay-per-ride pricing.

activities.name: Shingle Creek Regional Park
activities.description: Nature preserve at the headwaters of the Everglades featuring boardwalks, kayaking, fishing, and birdwatching.

activities.name: Kissimmee Lakefront Park
activities.description: Waterfront park on Lake Tohopekaliga with a fishing pier, playground, splash pad, and walking trails.

activities.name: Boggy Creek Airboat Adventures
activities.description: Nighttime and daytime airboat rides through Kissimmee's marshes spotting alligators, turtles, and wading birds.

activities.name: Lake Buena Vista Factory Stores
activities.description: Outlet shopping center convenient to the theme parks with brand-name stores at discounted prices.

activities.name: Reunion Resort Golf
activities.description: Three signature golf courses designed by Jack Nicklaus, Arnold Palmer, and Tom Watson at the Reunion Resort.

title: Things to Do in Melbourne
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Melbourne FL
pageSubtitle: Arts, nature, and coastal charm on Florida's Space Coast.
introContent: Melbourne is a charming Space Coast city with a vibrant downtown arts district, beautiful Indian River Lagoon frontage, and easy beach access. The Eau Gallie Arts District and historic downtown offer galleries, live music, and diverse dining.

activities.name: Eau Gallie Arts District
activities.description: Melbourne's creative hub featuring art galleries, studios, murals, and the monthly First Friday Art Walk.

activities.name: Melbourne Beach
activities.description: Quiet, uncrowded Atlantic beach popular with families, surfers, and sea turtle watchers during nesting season.

activities.name: Brevard Zoo
activities.description: Award-winning zoo with kayaking through animal exhibits, giraffe feeding, and the Treetop Trek aerial adventure course.

activities.name: Turkey Creek Sanctuary
activities.description: 130-acre nature preserve with a mile-long boardwalk through sand pine scrub habitat featuring gopher tortoises and migratory birds.

activities.name: Henegar Center for the Arts
activities.description: Historic theater hosting Broadway-style productions, concerts, and community performances in downtown Melbourne.

activities.name: Historic Downtown Melbourne
activities.description: Walkable downtown district along New Haven Avenue with restaurants, shops, breweries, and live music venues.

activities.name: Indian River Lagoon Kayaking
activities.description: Paddle through one of North America's most biodiverse estuaries, home to dolphins, manatees, and over 700 species of fish.

activities.name: Sebastian Inlet State Park
activities.description: Premier East Coast surfing spot and fishing destination where the Indian River meets the Atlantic Ocean.

activities.name: Viera Wetlands
activities.description: Constructed wetlands that have become a top birding destination in Florida, with over 200 species documented on the trails.

activities.name: Intracoastal Brewing Company
activities.description: Local craft brewery in downtown Melbourne with rotating taps, food trucks, and a dog-friendly outdoor patio.

title: Things to Do in Titusville
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Titusville FL
pageSubtitle: Gateway to Kennedy Space Center with wildlife, history, and waterfront charm.
introContent: Titusville is the closest city to Kennedy Space Center and offers some of the best rocket launch viewing spots in Florida. The charming downtown, Indian River Lagoon frontage, and Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge make it a nature-lover's paradise.

activities.name: Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex
activities.description: The world's premier space exploration attraction, featuring the Space Shuttle Atlantis, Saturn V rocket, and astronaut experiences.

activities.name: Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge
activities.description: 140,000 acres of protected habitat with the 7-mile Black Point Wildlife Drive, hiking trails, and diverse bird species.

activities.name: Space View Park
activities.description: Titusville's famous launch viewing park on the Indian River with unobstructed views of Kennedy Space Center launch pads.

activities.name: Playalinda Brewing Company
activities.description: Craft brewery in a restored downtown building with space-themed beers, wood-fired pizzas, and a vibrant taproom.

activities.name: Playalinda Beach
activities.description: Pristine, undeveloped beach in Canaveral National Seashore - one of the most natural beach experiences in Florida.

activities.name: Birding on the Space Coast
activities.description: The Space Coast is home to over 360 bird species, making it one of the top birding destinations in North America.

activities.name: Historic Downtown Titusville
activities.description: Walkable downtown with local shops, restaurants, the Titusville Playhouse, and a weekly farmers market.

activities.name: Indian River Lagoon Fishing
activities.description: World-class redfish, trout, and snook fishing in the most biodiverse estuary in North America.

activities.name: Haulover Canal Kayaking
activities.description: Paddle through mangrove-lined canals where manatees gather in winter months within the Merritt Island refuge.

activities.name: American Space Museum
activities.description: Small museum in downtown Titusville dedicated to the history of America's space program with astronaut memorabilia and artifacts.

title: Things to Do in Winter Park
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Winter Park FL
pageSubtitle: Boutique shopping, scenic boat tours, and fine dining in Orlando's most charming neighborhood.
introContent: Winter Park is a picturesque city just north of Orlando known for its tree-lined streets, chain of lakes, and upscale Park Avenue shopping district. Home to Rollins College, world-class museums, and some of Central Florida's best dining.

activities.name: Park Avenue
activities.description: Charming tree-lined street with upscale boutiques, restaurants, sidewalk cafes, and Central Park's rose garden.

activities.name: Scenic Boat Tour
activities.description: Narrated pontoon boat tour through Winter Park's chain of lakes and canals, passing mansions, Rollins College, and wildlife since 1938.

activities.name: Charles Hosmer Morse Museum of American Art
activities.description: World's most comprehensive collection of Louis Comfort Tiffany works, including the restored Tiffany Chapel from the 1893 World's Fair.

activities.name: Mead Botanical Garden
activities.description: 48-acre garden with nature trails, butterfly garden, and a historic greenhouse in the heart of Winter Park.

activities.name: Enzian Theater
activities.description: Independent art house cinema serving food and drinks, host of the annual Florida Film Festival.

activities.name: Rollins College
activities.description: Beautiful lakefront liberal arts college with Spanish Mediterranean architecture, the Cornell Fine Arts Museum, and public events.

activities.name: Winter Park Farmers Market
activities.description: Saturday morning market at the old train depot with local produce, baked goods, plants, and prepared foods.

activities.name: The Ravenous Pig
activities.description: James Beard-nominated gastropub serving elevated American fare with an on-site brewery - one of Central Florida's best restaurants.

activities.name: Alfond Inn Art Collection
activities.description: Boutique hotel housing an impressive collection of contemporary art from the Alfond Collection at Rollins College.

activities.name: Dinky Dock Park
activities.description: Lakefront park on Lake Virginia with kayak and paddleboard rentals, perfect for a relaxing afternoon on the water.

title: Things to Do in Sarasota
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do in Sarasota FL
pageSubtitle: World-class beaches, circus history, art museums, and Gulf Coast culture.
introContent: Sarasota is a Gulf Coast gem known for Siesta Key's world-famous quartz sand beach, the Ringling Museum, and a thriving arts community. The city blends natural beauty with cultural sophistication, offering everything from opera to kayaking.

activities.name: Siesta Key Beach
activities.description: Consistently ranked the best beach in America, featuring powdery quartz sand that stays cool underfoot and crystal-clear Gulf waters.

activities.name: The Ringling
activities.description: 66-acre cultural complex including the Museum of Art, Circus Museum, Ca d'Zan mansion, and Bayfront Gardens.

activities.name: Marie Selby Botanical Gardens
activities.description: 15-acre bayfront garden specializing in epiphytes and orchids, with a stunning glass greenhouse and tropical trails.

activities.name: St. Armands Circle
activities.description: Upscale circular shopping district on a barrier island with boutiques, galleries, restaurants, and a European plaza feel.

activities.name: Mote Marine Laboratory & Aquarium
activities.description: Marine research facility and aquarium on Longboat Key with shark exhibits, manatee research, and sea turtle rehabilitation.

activities.name: Longboat Key
activities.description: Exclusive barrier island with pristine Gulf beaches, upscale resorts, and a quiet, uncrowded atmosphere.

activities.name: Sarasota Opera House
activities.description: Historic Mediterranean Revival opera house hosting world-class opera, ballet, and musical performances.

activities.name: Myakka River State Park
activities.description: 37,000-acre park with airboat tours, canopy walkway, hiking, and abundant wildlife including alligators and wild boar.

activities.name: Snorkeling at Point of Rocks
activities.description: Rocky outcrops on Siesta Key's south end offering the best shore snorkeling on Florida's Gulf Coast.

activities.name: Bayfront Park
activities.description: Scenic waterfront park along Sarasota Bay with walking paths, playgrounds, and stunning sunset views of the bay.

title: Things to Do in Lakeland
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Lakeland FL
pageSubtitle: Frank Lloyd Wright architecture, lakes, and small-town Florida charm between Tampa and Orlando.
introContent: Lakeland sits between Tampa and Orlando and is home to the largest collection of Frank Lloyd Wright buildings in the world at Florida Southern College. The city features beautiful lakefront parks, the charming Dixieland district, and a thriving local food scene.

activities.name: Florida Southern College - Frank Lloyd Wright
activities.description: The largest single-site collection of Frank Lloyd Wright architecture in the world, with guided tours of 13 structures on campus.

activities.name: Lake Morton
activities.description: Downtown lake famous for its resident swans and ducks, surrounded by historic homes and a scenic walking path.

activities.name: Polk Museum of Art
activities.description: Regional art museum featuring American and Asian art collections, rotating exhibitions, and free admission.

activities.name: Dixieland Historic District
activities.description: Walkable neighborhood with craftsman bungalows, antique shops, cafes, and a monthly art walk.

activities.name: Publix Field at Joker Marchant Stadium
activities.description: Spring training home of the Detroit Tigers, hosting Grapefruit League games and baseball events.

activities.name: Circle B Bar Reserve
activities.description: 1,267-acre nature preserve with hiking trails through wetlands, oak hammocks, and abundant wildlife including alligators.

activities.name: Swan Brewing
activities.description: Downtown Lakeland craft brewery in a converted historic building with rotating taps, food trucks, and live music.

activities.name: Lakeland Downtown Farmers Curb Market
activities.description: Saturday morning market with local produce, baked goods, plants, and artisan crafts in Munn Park.

activities.name: Polk Theatre
activities.description: Beautifully restored 1928 Art Deco theater hosting concerts, movies, comedy, and community events.

activities.name: Hollis Garden
activities.description: 1.2-acre formal garden on the shores of Lake Mirror featuring over 10,000 flowers, fountains, and sculptures.

title: Things to Do in Sanford
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Sanford FL
pageSubtitle: Historic riverfront charm, craft beer, and nature adventures north of Orlando.
introContent: Sanford is a revitalized historic city on the shores of Lake Monroe, about 25 minutes north of Orlando. The charming downtown features craft breweries, antique shops, and the famous Hollerbach's German restaurant, all set along a scenic riverfront.

activities.name: Historic Downtown Sanford
activities.description: Walkable downtown with restored brick buildings housing breweries, restaurants, antique shops, and art galleries.

activities.name: Sanford Craft Beer Scene
activities.description: Downtown Sanford has become a craft beer destination with Wop's Hops, Celery City Craft, Inner Compass Brewing, and more.

activities.name: Hollerbach's Willow Tree Cafe
activities.description: Authentic German restaurant and biergarten with live polka music, communal tables, and traditional Bavarian cuisine.

activities.name: Sanford RiverWalk
activities.description: Scenic waterfront walkway along Lake Monroe with parks, public art, and views of the marina and St. Johns River.

activities.name: Black Bear Wilderness Area
activities.description: 1,600-acre nature preserve with a 7.1-mile loop trail through wetlands and hardwood hammocks along the St. Johns River.

activities.name: St. Johns River Eco Tours
activities.description: Boat tours on the St. Johns River spotting alligators, bald eagles, manatees, and native Florida wildlife.

activities.name: Sanford Museum
activities.description: Local history museum in a 1902 building telling the story of Sanford from its founding as a steamboat port through the celery boom.

activities.name: Sanford Antique Row
activities.description: Collection of antique malls and vintage shops along 1st Street, making Sanford one of Central Florida's best antiquing destinations.

activities.name: Lake Monroe Sunset
activities.description: The Lake Monroe waterfront offers some of the most spectacular sunset views in Central Florida from the marina and Fort Mellon Park.

activities.name: Mayfair Country Club
activities.description: Historic golf course and country club offering 18 holes in a scenic lakeside setting near downtown Sanford.

title: Things to Do in Palm Beach
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Palm Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Luxury shopping, world-class museums, and pristine beaches on the Gold Coast.
introContent: Palm Beach is one of America's wealthiest communities, known for Worth Avenue's luxury shopping, The Breakers resort, and stunning Atlantic beaches. The island town offers world-class museums, fine dining, and an elegant tropical atmosphere year-round.

activities.name: Worth Avenue
activities.description: Palm Beach's legendary luxury shopping street with designer boutiques, art galleries, and Mediterranean-style courtyards.

activities.name: The Breakers Palm Beach
activities.description: Legendary 1896 Italian Renaissance-style oceanfront resort with dining, spa, golf, and public tours available.

activities.name: Henry Morrison Flagler Museum
activities.description: Gilded Age mansion built in 1902 by Standard Oil co-founder Henry Flagler, with period furnishings and a private railcar.

activities.name: Palm Beach Municipal Beach
activities.description: Beautiful public beach on the Atlantic with free parking, lifeguards, and the iconic Palm Beach skyline.

activities.name: Norton Museum of Art
activities.description: World-class art museum in West Palm Beach featuring works by Monet, Picasso, and an extensive photography collection.

activities.name: Pan's Garden
activities.description: One-acre native plant garden maintained by the Preservation Foundation of Palm Beach showcasing Florida's native flora.

activities.name: Lake Trail
activities.description: Scenic 5.5-mile lakeside trail along the Intracoastal Waterway, popular for biking, walking, and jogging.

activities.name: Loggerhead Marinelife Center
activities.description: Sea turtle conservation center in Juno Beach with rehabilitation hospital, exhibits, and guided beach walks during nesting season.

activities.name: PGA National Resort
activities.description: Premier golf resort in Palm Beach Gardens with five championship courses including The Champion, host of the Honda Classic.

activities.name: Kravis Center for the Performing Arts
activities.description: Major performing arts venue in West Palm Beach hosting Broadway, ballet, opera, and concerts.

title: Things to Do in Naples
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Naples FL
pageSubtitle: Pristine Gulf beaches, upscale dining, and Everglades adventures on Florida's Paradise Coast.
introContent: Naples is a sophisticated Gulf Coast city known for its pristine white-sand beaches, world-class golf courses, and proximity to the Everglades. Fifth Avenue South and Third Street South offer upscale shopping and dining in a charming downtown setting.

activities.name: Naples Beach & Pier
activities.description: Stunning white-sand Gulf beach with the iconic Naples Pier, one of the most photographed spots on Florida's west coast.

activities.name: Naples Botanical Garden
activities.description: 170-acre world-class botanical garden featuring themed gardens from around the tropics, the Scott Florida Garden, and nature trails.

activities.name: Everglades National Park
activities.description: The largest subtropical wilderness in the US, accessible from Naples via Shark Valley and Gulf Coast entrances with airboat tours and hiking.

activities.name: Fifth Avenue South
activities.description: Naples' premier shopping and dining street with upscale boutiques, galleries, restaurants, and the charming Sugden Community Theatre.

activities.name: Delnor-Wiggins Pass State Park
activities.description: Mile-long pristine beach consistently ranked among Florida's best, with excellent shelling, snorkeling, and kayaking.

activities.name: Baker Museum at Artis-Naples
activities.description: Contemporary art museum featuring over 4,500 works including Chihuly glass, modern paintings, and sculpture in a stunning building.

activities.name: Naples Dolphin & Shelling Tour
activities.description: Boat tours departing from Tin City to see dolphins and explore barrier islands for shelling on the Gulf.

activities.name: Corkscrew Swamp Sanctuary
activities.description: Audubon sanctuary with a 2.25-mile boardwalk through the largest remaining old-growth bald cypress forest in North America.

activities.name: Tiburon Golf Club
activities.description: 36-hole Greg Norman-designed championship golf facility at The Ritz-Carlton Naples, host of the LPGA Tour.

activities.name: Third Street South
activities.description: Charming shopping and dining district with art galleries, boutiques, restaurants, and a weekly farmers market.

title: Things to Do in Key West
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do in Key West FL
pageSubtitle: Sunset celebrations, historic landmarks, and island culture at the end of the road.
introContent: Key West is the southernmost point of the continental United States, a free-spirited island city known for its vibrant nightlife, literary history, coral reefs, and nightly sunset celebrations at Mallory Square. Getting there is half the adventure via the Overseas Highway.

activities.name: Mallory Square Sunset Celebration
activities.description: Nightly festival two hours before sunset with street performers, artists, food vendors, and spectacular sunsets over the Gulf.

activities.name: Ernest Hemingway Home & Museum
activities.description: The home where Hemingway wrote many of his greatest works, featuring polydactyl cats descended from his original pets.

activities.name: Dry Tortugas National Park
activities.description: Remote island park accessible by boat or seaplane, featuring Fort Jefferson, pristine snorkeling, and incredible blue waters.

activities.name: Key West Snorkeling & Diving
activities.description: North America's only living coral barrier reef offers world-class snorkeling and diving from Key West.

activities.name: Duval Street
activities.description: Key West's main street running from the Gulf to the Atlantic, lined with bars, restaurants, shops, and galleries.

activities.name: Southernmost Point Buoy
activities.description: The iconic concrete buoy marking the southernmost point of the continental US - just 90 miles from Cuba.

activities.name: Harry S. Truman Little White House
activities.description: Truman's winter White House where he spent 175 working days during his presidency, with guided tours.

activities.name: Key West Butterfly & Nature Conservatory
activities.description: Glass-enclosed habitat with over 50 butterfly species, exotic birds, and lush tropical gardens.

activities.name: Key West Bar Crawl
activities.description: Legendary bar scene including Sloppy Joe's, Captain Tony's, The Green Parrot, and the original Jimmy Buffett's Margaritaville.

activities.name: Key West Sailing & Kayaking
activities.description: Sunset sails, kayak tours through mangrove islands, and jet ski tours of the Key West harbor and backcountry.

title: Things to Do in Miami
pageHeading: 40 Best Things to Do in Miami FL
pageSubtitle: Art Deco, beaches, Latin culture, and world-class nightlife in the Magic City.
introContent: Miami is a global city where Latin American culture, Art Deco architecture, and stunning beaches converge. From the colorful murals of Wynwood to the glamorous shores of South Beach, Miami offers an energy unlike anywhere else in Florida.

activities.name: South Beach
activities.description: Iconic Miami Beach with Art Deco architecture along Ocean Drive, turquoise waters, and a vibrant beachfront scene.

activities.name: Wynwood Walls
activities.description: World-famous outdoor street art museum in the Wynwood Arts District featuring murals by internationally acclaimed artists.

activities.name: Art Deco Historic District
activities.description: The largest collection of Art Deco architecture in the world along South Beach's Ocean Drive and Collins Avenue.

activities.name: Vizcaya Museum & Gardens
activities.description: Stunning Italian Renaissance-style villa and gardens built in 1916 on Biscayne Bay, with European art and lush formal gardens.

activities.name: Key Biscayne & Crandon Park
activities.description: Barrier island with pristine beaches, the historic Cape Florida Lighthouse, and Crandon Park's turquoise waters.

activities.name: Little Havana - Calle Ocho
activities.description: The heart of Cuban-American culture in Miami with Cuban coffee windows, Domino Park, cigar shops, and authentic Latin restaurants.

activities.name: Miami Seaquarium
activities.description: Marine park on Virginia Key with dolphin shows, manatee encounters, and a coral reef aquarium.

activities.name: Biscayne National Park
activities.description: 95% underwater national park protecting coral reefs, mangroves, and keys - accessible by boat from Homestead.

activities.name: Design District
activities.description: Luxury shopping and dining neighborhood with flagship stores, contemporary art galleries, and architecture showpieces.

activities.name: Miami Nightlife - South Beach
activities.description: World-famous nightlife scene with clubs like LIV, Story, and E11EVEN, plus rooftop bars and live music venues.

activities.name: Everglades National Park
activities.description: 1.5 million acres of subtropical wilderness with airboat tours, the Anhinga Trail, and diverse wildlife including manatees and panthers.

activities.name: Perez Art Museum Miami (PAMM)
activities.description: Waterfront contemporary art museum on Biscayne Bay designed by Herzog & de Meuron, with rotating exhibitions and bay views.

title: Things to Do in Lake Buena Vista
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Lake Buena Vista FL
pageSubtitle: Theme parks, dining, and entertainment at the heart of the Disney World area.
introContent: Lake Buena Vista is home to Walt Disney World Resort and Disney Springs, making it the epicenter of Central Florida's theme park corridor. Beyond Disney, the area offers shopping, dining, golf, and entertainment for every age group.

activities.name: Walt Disney World - Magic Kingdom
activities.description: The most visited theme park in the world featuring Cinderella Castle, Space Mountain, Pirates of the Caribbean, and classic Disney magic.

activities.name: EPCOT
activities.description: Disney's experimental community of tomorrow featuring Future World attractions, World Showcase with 11 country pavilions, and seasonal festivals.

activities.name: Hollywood Studios
activities.description: Movie and TV-themed park featuring Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, Toy Story Land, and Tower of Terror.

activities.name: Animal Kingdom
activities.description: Nature-themed park combining rides like Expedition Everest and Avatar Flight of Passage with real animal encounters.

activities.name: Disney Springs
activities.description: Open-air shopping, dining, and entertainment complex with over 100 venues, no park admission required.

activities.name: Walt Disney World Golf
activities.description: Four championship golf courses at Disney including the Palm, Magnolia, and Lake Buena Vista courses.

activities.name: Disney Resort Spas
activities.description: Full-service spas at the Grand Floridian, Saratoga Springs, and other deluxe Disney resorts open to all guests.

activities.name: Splitsville Luxury Lanes
activities.description: Upscale bowling, billiards, and dining at Disney Springs with a rooftop patio and full restaurant menu.

activities.name: Orlando Premium Outlets Vineland
activities.description: Large outlet mall near Disney Springs with over 150 brand-name stores at discounted prices.

activities.name: Disney's Typhoon Lagoon
activities.description: Tropical-themed water park with a massive wave pool, water slides, and the Crush 'n' Gusher water coaster.

title: Things to Do in Ocala
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Ocala FL
pageSubtitle: Horse country, natural springs, and outdoor adventures in Central Florida's heartland.
introContent: Ocala is the Horse Capital of the World, with over 1,200 horse farms and training centers. The area also features some of Florida's most beautiful natural springs, the Ocala National Forest, and Silver Springs - one of the state's original tourist attractions.

activities.name: Horse Farm Tours
activities.description: Visit world-class thoroughbred farms in Marion County, the Horse Capital of the World with over 1,200 farms.

activities.name: Silver Springs State Park
activities.description: Florida's original tourist attraction featuring glass-bottom boat tours over crystal-clear springs and nature trails.

activities.name: Ocala National Forest
activities.description: The southernmost forest in the continental US with springs, hiking, camping, and the Juniper Springs canoe run.

activities.name: Rainbow Springs State Park
activities.description: First-magnitude spring with crystal-clear swimming area, tubing run, waterfalls, and nature trails.

activities.name: Santos Trailhead Mountain Biking
activities.description: One of the top mountain biking destinations in the Southeast with over 85 miles of trails through the forest.

activities.name: Juniper Springs Canoe Run
activities.description: 7-mile paddle through a lush subtropical jungle in the Ocala National Forest - one of Florida's most scenic waterways.

activities.name: Downtown Ocala Square
activities.description: Historic downtown centered around the courthouse square with restaurants, shops, art galleries, and community events.

activities.name: Appleton Museum of Art
activities.description: World-class art museum at the College of Central Florida featuring European, American, Asian, and African collections.

activities.name: Alexander Springs
activities.description: Crystal-clear first-magnitude spring in the Ocala National Forest with swimming, snorkeling, canoeing, and a nature trail.

activities.name: World Equestrian Center
activities.description: State-of-the-art equestrian facility hosting national and international horse shows year-round with dining and shopping.

title: Things to Do in Gainesville
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Gainesville FL
pageSubtitle: University culture, natural springs, and outdoor recreation in North Central Florida.
introContent: Gainesville is home to the University of Florida and offers a unique blend of college-town energy, natural springs, and cultural attractions. The city is a gateway to some of Florida's best freshwater springs and nature preserves.

activities.name: University of Florida
activities.description: One of the largest universities in the US with the Florida Museum of Natural History, Butterfly Rainforest, and Gator football.

activities.name: Florida Museum of Natural History
activities.description: Free museum on the UF campus with the Butterfly Rainforest, fossil exhibits, and Florida habitat displays.

activities.name: Ichetucknee Springs State Park
activities.description: Crystal-clear spring-fed river perfect for tubing, swimming, and snorkeling through a pristine Florida ecosystem.

activities.name: Ginnie Springs
activities.description: Private spring park famous for cave diving, crystal-clear swimming, tubing on the Santa Fe River, and camping.

activities.name: Ben Hill Griffin Stadium (The Swamp)
activities.description: Home of the Florida Gators, seating nearly 90,000 fans - one of the most iconic college football venues in America.

activities.name: Paynes Prairie Preserve State Park
activities.description: 21,000-acre savanna with bison, wild horses, alligators, and over 20 miles of trails south of Gainesville.

activities.name: Hippodrome Theatre
activities.description: Professional regional theater in a restored 1911 federal building hosting plays, films, and gallery exhibitions.

activities.name: Downtown Gainesville
activities.description: Walkable downtown with live music venues, farm-to-table restaurants, independent bookshops, and the Bo Diddley Plaza.

activities.name: UF Bat Houses
activities.description: The largest occupied bat houses in North America, home to over 500,000 bats - watch the nightly flyout at sunset.

activities.name: Santa Fe River Paddling
activities.description: Paddle the spring-fed Santa Fe River through limestone karst terrain with swimming holes and natural bridges.

title: Things to Do in New Smyrna Beach
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in New Smyrna Beach FL
pageSubtitle: East Coast surfing, art galleries, and casual beach town vibes south of Daytona.
introContent: New Smyrna Beach is one of the East Coast's best surfing destinations with a charming arts scene on Flagler Avenue and Canal Street. The laid-back beach town sits at the southern gateway to Canaveral National Seashore.

activities.name: Surfing at the Inlet
activities.description: New Smyrna's jetty creates consistent waves that make it one of the top surfing spots on the East Coast year-round.

activities.name: New Smyrna Beach
activities.description: Miles of drivable beach with soft sand, warm Atlantic waters, and fewer crowds than nearby Daytona Beach.

activities.name: Flagler Avenue
activities.description: The heart of NSB with surf shops, art galleries, craft breweries, restaurants, and a Saturday morning farmers market.

activities.name: Canal Street Historic District
activities.description: Arts and shopping district with galleries, antique stores, and the monthly Art Walk featuring local artists.

activities.name: Canaveral National Seashore - South District
activities.description: Apollo Beach offers 6 miles of undeveloped shoreline, turtle nesting grounds, and the Turtle Mound archaeological site.

activities.name: Indian River Lagoon Kayaking
activities.description: Paddle through the most biodiverse estuary in North America spotting dolphins, manatees, and over 700 fish species.

activities.name: Smyrna Dunes Park
activities.description: 73-acre park on the peninsula between the Indian River and Atlantic with 2 miles of elevated boardwalks and great birding.

activities.name: New Smyrna Beach Brewing Company
activities.description: Local craft brewery on Canal Street with rotating taps, food trucks, and a dog-friendly patio.

activities.name: NSB Fishing
activities.description: Excellent inshore and offshore fishing from the jetty, Indian River bridges, and charter boats targeting redfish, snook, and tarpon.

activities.name: Marine Discovery Center
activities.description: Environmental education center offering eco-tours, dolphin watching, and guided kayak trips through the Indian River Lagoon.

title: Things to Do in Jupiter
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Jupiter FL
pageSubtitle: Lighthouse views, natural beaches, and tropical dining on the Treasure Coast.
introContent: Jupiter is a beautiful coastal town on Florida's Treasure Coast known for its iconic red lighthouse, pristine beaches, and lush natural areas. Spring training baseball, waterfront dining, and wildlife encounters make it a popular destination.

activities.name: Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse
activities.description: Historic 1860 lighthouse with panoramic views from the top, a museum, and nature trails through the Jupiter Inlet Outstanding Natural Area.

activities.name: Jupiter Beach
activities.description: Dog-friendly beach with natural dunes, sea turtle nesting, and a relaxed atmosphere away from South Florida's bustle.

activities.name: Roger Dean Chevrolet Stadium
activities.description: Spring training home of the Miami Marlins and St. Louis Cardinals, hosting Grapefruit League games each February and March.

activities.name: Guanabanas
activities.description: Unique waterfront restaurant set under massive banyan trees with Caribbean cuisine, live music, and a tropical garden atmosphere.

activities.name: Jonathan Dickinson State Park
activities.description: 11,500-acre park with hiking, kayaking, mountain biking, the Loxahatchee River, and the Trapper Nelson historic site.

activities.name: Loggerhead Marinelife Center
activities.description: Sea turtle hospital and education center in nearby Juno Beach with rehabilitation exhibits and guided beach walks.

activities.name: Loxahatchee River Kayaking
activities.description: Florida's only federally designated Wild and Scenic River with guided kayak tours through cypress swamps.

activities.name: Snorkeling at Blowing Rocks Preserve
activities.description: Unique limestone outcrop along the beach where waves blow through holes in the rock - great snorkeling and tide pooling.

activities.name: Jupiter Hills Club
activities.description: Prestigious private golf club, but nearby public courses like Abacoa and Jupiter Dunes offer excellent rounds.

activities.name: Harbourside Place
activities.description: Waterfront shopping and dining district on the Intracoastal with restaurants, boutiques, and live entertainment.

title: Things to Do in Stuart
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do in Stuart FL
pageSubtitle: The Sailfish Capital of the World offers small-town charm and outdoor adventures.
introContent: Stuart is a charming waterfront town on the Treasure Coast known as the Sailfish Capital of the World. The walkable downtown along the St. Lucie River features local shops, restaurants, and a relaxed Florida atmosphere.

activities.name: Offshore Fishing
activities.description: World-class sailfish, marlin, and deep-sea fishing charters depart from Stuart's marinas into the Gulf Stream.

activities.name: Downtown Stuart
activities.description: Charming walkable downtown along the St. Lucie River with galleries, boutiques, restaurants, and live music.

activities.name: Bathtub Reef Beach
activities.description: Unique beach with a natural reef formation creating a calm, shallow wading area - perfect for families with small children.

activities.name: Hobe Sound National Wildlife Refuge
activities.description: Over 1,000 acres of protected coastal habitat with sea turtle nesting beaches and nature trails.

activities.name: House of Refuge
activities.description: The only remaining House of Refuge on the East Coast, built in 1876 for shipwrecked sailors, now a maritime museum.

activities.name: St. Lucie River Paddling
activities.description: Kayak or paddleboard the St. Lucie River and Indian River Lagoon through mangrove tunnels spotting manatees and dolphins.

activities.name: Florida Oceanographic Coastal Center
activities.description: Marine education center on Hutchinson Island with stingray touch tanks, sea turtle hospital, and nature walks.

activities.name: Stuart Green Market
activities.description: Sunday morning farmers market in downtown Stuart with local produce, baked goods, crafts, and live music.

activities.name: Jensen Beach
activities.description: Quiet beach community just north of Stuart with uncrowded shores, a fishing pier, and sea turtle nesting.

activities.name: Sailfish Splash Waterpark
activities.description: County waterpark with slides, a lazy river, and a splash area - a fun family activity on the Treasure Coast.

title: Things to Do in Vero Beach
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do in Vero Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Quiet beaches, botanical gardens, and cultural treasures on the Treasure Coast.
introContent: Vero Beach is a laid-back Treasure Coast community known for uncrowded beaches, the stunning McKee Botanical Garden, and spring training baseball. The oceanside village on Ocean Drive and downtown arts district offer charming shops and restaurants.

activities.name: Vero Beach Oceanside
activities.description: Miles of quiet, uncrowded Atlantic beach with the charming Ocean Drive shopping village steps from the sand.

activities.name: McKee Botanical Garden
activities.description: 18-acre tropical garden featuring a National Historic Landmark with water lily ponds, a children's garden, and seasonal exhibits.

activities.name: Clover Park
activities.description: Spring training home of the New York Mets with Grapefruit League games in February and March.

activities.name: Vero Beach Museum of Art
activities.description: Largest art museum on the Treasure Coast with rotating exhibitions, sculpture garden, and art education programs.

activities.name: Sebastian Inlet State Park
activities.description: Premier fishing and surfing spot where the Indian River meets the Atlantic with camping, nature trails, and a museum.

activities.name: Round Island Beach Park
activities.description: Beautiful beach park at the south end of the island with boardwalks, picnic areas, and sea turtle nesting.

activities.name: Environmental Learning Center
activities.description: 64-acre island nature center with kayak tours, nature walks, and exhibits about the Indian River Lagoon ecosystem.

activities.name: Ocean Drive
activities.description: Charming beachside shopping district with boutiques, cafes, and the Driftwood Resort - a Vero Beach landmark.

activities.name: Riverside Theatre
activities.description: Professional regional theater in downtown Vero Beach hosting Broadway-quality productions, concerts, and comedy.

activities.name: Sea Turtle Nesting Walks
activities.description: Guided nighttime beach walks to observe endangered sea turtles nesting on Vero's beaches from June through July.

title: Things to Do in DeLand
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do in DeLand FL
pageSubtitle: Skydiving capital, natural springs, and charming small-town Florida.
introContent: DeLand is the Skydiving Capital of the World and home to Stetson University, Florida's oldest private university. The walkable downtown features locally owned shops and restaurants, while nearby springs and parks offer natural adventures.

activities.name: Skydive DeLand
activities.description: The Skydiving Capital of the World with tandem jumps, solo certification courses, and a vibrant drop zone community.

activities.name: DeLeon Springs State Park
activities.description: Natural spring with a swim area and the Old Spanish Sugar Mill pancake house where you cook your own pancakes at the table.

activities.name: Downtown DeLand
activities.description: Walkable Main Street America town with local shops, restaurants, breweries, and the historic Athens Theatre.

activities.name: Stetson University
activities.description: Florida's oldest private university with a beautiful campus featuring historic buildings, the Gillespie Museum, and a nature trail.

activities.name: Blue Spring State Park
activities.description: One of Florida's best manatee viewing locations in winter, with a crystal-clear spring run for swimming and snorkeling.

activities.name: Athens Theatre
activities.description: Restored 1921 vaudeville theater hosting concerts, plays, comedy, and community events in downtown DeLand.

activities.name: Spring-to-Spring Trail
activities.description: 26-mile paved trail connecting DeLeon Springs to Gemini Springs through scenic Florida landscapes.

activities.name: Persimmon Hollow Brewing
activities.description: Downtown DeLand craft brewery in a restored building with rotating taps, food trucks, and a friendly taproom.

activities.name: Museum of Art - DeLand
activities.description: Visual arts museum with rotating exhibitions, a permanent collection, and community art events.

activities.name: St. Johns River Eco Tours
activities.description: Boat and kayak tours on the St. Johns River from nearby Blue Spring and Hontoon Island State Parks.

title: Things to Do in Fort Myers
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Fort Myers FL
pageSubtitle: Edison's winter estate, shell-covered islands, and Gulf Coast beaches.
introContent: Fort Myers is a Gulf Coast city where the Caloosahatchee River meets the Gulf of Mexico. Thomas Edison and Henry Ford both had winter estates here, and the nearby barrier islands of Sanibel and Captiva are world-famous for shelling.

activities.name: Edison and Ford Winter Estates
activities.description: 20-acre historical site featuring Thomas Edison's and Henry Ford's winter homes, botanical gardens, museum, and laboratory.

activities.name: Sanibel Island
activities.description: World-renowned shelling destination with pristine beaches, the J.N. Ding Darling National Wildlife Refuge, and a charming village.

activities.name: Fort Myers Beach
activities.description: Lively beach town on Estero Island with waterfront dining, fishing pier, and calm Gulf waters.

activities.name: J.N. Ding Darling NWR
activities.description: 6,400-acre wildlife refuge on Sanibel Island with a scenic drive, kayaking, and over 245 bird species.

activities.name: Downtown Fort Myers River District
activities.description: Revitalized waterfront downtown with restaurants, galleries, the Florida Repertory Theatre, and monthly Art Walk.

activities.name: Captiva Island
activities.description: Secluded island with luxury resorts, pristine beaches, and spectacular Gulf sunsets accessible through Sanibel.

activities.name: Pine Island Fishing
activities.description: World-class tarpon, snook, and redfish fishing in Pine Island Sound and Charlotte Harbor.

activities.name: Raptor Bay Golf Club
activities.description: Championship Raymond Floyd-designed course in Bonita Springs near Fort Myers.

activities.name: Great Calusa Blueway
activities.description: 190-mile paddling trail through Lee County's coastal waterways connecting beaches, islands, and wildlife areas.

activities.name: Florida Repertory Theatre
activities.description: Professional regional theater in the historic Arcade Theatre in downtown Fort Myers.

title: Things to Do in Pensacola
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Pensacola FL
pageSubtitle: Sugar-white beaches, naval aviation history, and Gulf Coast culture.
introContent: Pensacola is Florida's westernmost city with stunning emerald-green Gulf waters, white quartz sand beaches, and a rich military and maritime heritage. The National Naval Aviation Museum and Pensacola Beach are world-class attractions.

activities.name: National Naval Aviation Museum
activities.description: Free museum featuring over 150 restored aircraft, IMAX theater, flight simulators, and the Blue Angels practice field.

activities.name: Pensacola Beach
activities.description: Sugar-white quartz sand and emerald Gulf waters on Santa Rosa Island, consistently ranked among America's top beaches.

activities.name: Historic Pensacola Village
activities.description: 27-acre historic district with preserved 18th and 19th century buildings, museums, and costumed interpreters.

activities.name: Fort Pickens
activities.description: Massive Civil War-era fort on the western tip of Santa Rosa Island with beaches, camping, and nature trails.

activities.name: Palafox Street
activities.description: Downtown Pensacola's vibrant main street with restaurants, bars, galleries, and the Saenger Theatre.

activities.name: Blue Angels Practice Shows
activities.description: Watch the Navy's famous Blue Angels practice at NAS Pensacola on select Tuesdays and Wednesdays - free and spectacular.

activities.name: Gulf Islands National Seashore
activities.description: 160 miles of protected barrier islands with pristine beaches, historic forts, and nature trails.

activities.name: Pensacola Craft Beer Trail
activities.description: Thriving craft beer scene with Perfect Plain, Odd Colony, Pensacola Bay Brewery, and more in walkable downtown.

activities.name: Dolphin Cruises
activities.description: Boat tours from Pensacola Beach and downtown marinas to spot dolphins in the Gulf and Pensacola Bay.

activities.name: Pensacola Fishing
activities.description: Deep-sea fishing in the Gulf targeting red snapper, grouper, and amberjack from the Pensacola Pass.

title: Things to Do in Destin
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Destin FL
pageSubtitle: Emerald waters, world-class fishing, and family fun on the Emerald Coast.
introContent: Destin is known as the World's Luckiest Fishing Village and sits on the Emerald Coast in Florida's panhandle. The stunning emerald-green waters, sugar-white sand beaches, and Destin Harbor boardwalk make it one of Florida's top vacation destinations.

activities.name: Henderson Beach State Park
activities.description: Pristine beach with towering sand dunes, emerald waters, nature trails, and a boardwalk through coastal scrub habitat.

activities.name: Destin Harbor Fishing Charters
activities.description: The World's Luckiest Fishing Village offers deep-sea, inshore, and bay fishing trips from the Destin Harbor boardwalk.

activities.name: HarborWalk Village
activities.description: Waterfront entertainment district on Destin Harbor with restaurants, shops, a zip line, and fireworks shows.

activities.name: Dolphin Cruises
activities.description: Scenic boat tours from Destin Harbor into the Gulf and Choctawhatchee Bay to see dolphins in the emerald waters.

activities.name: Crab Island
activities.description: Famous sandbar in the Choctawhatchee Bay accessible only by boat, with floating vendors, water trampolines, and crystal-clear water.

activities.name: Big Kahuna's Water & Adventure Park
activities.description: Water park and adventure park with water slides, a lazy river, go-karts, and mini golf.

activities.name: Destin Snorkeling
activities.description: Crystal-clear emerald waters with visibility up to 80 feet - snorkel from shore or take a boat to the jetties and reefs.

activities.name: Destin Commons
activities.description: Open-air lifestyle center with national retailers, restaurants, a movie theater, and family entertainment.

activities.name: Regatta Bay Golf Club
activities.description: Championship golf course with panoramic views of the Choctawhatchee Bay designed by Robert Walker.

activities.name: Grayton Beach State Park
activities.description: Beautiful coastal dune lake beach, repeatedly voted America's best beach, just east of Destin in the 30A area.

title: Things to Do in Panama City Beach
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Panama City Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Gulf beaches, water parks, and family entertainment on Florida's Panhandle.
introContent: Panama City Beach is a popular Gulf Coast destination known for 27 miles of white-sand beaches, Pier Park entertainment, and Shell Island's undeveloped beauty. The warm Gulf waters and family-friendly attractions make it a perennial vacation favorite.

activities.name: Panama City Beach
activities.description: 27 miles of sugar-white sand and emerald Gulf waters, with the City Pier offering fishing and sunset views.

activities.name: Shell Island
activities.description: 700-acre undeveloped barrier island accessible by boat with pristine beaches, snorkeling, and dolphin sightings.

activities.name: Pier Park
activities.description: Massive beachfront shopping and entertainment complex with over 120 stores, restaurants, an IMAX theater, and SkyWheel.

activities.name: SkyWheel Panama City Beach
activities.description: 200-foot observation wheel at Pier Park with panoramic views of the Gulf Coast in climate-controlled gondolas.

activities.name: Gulf World Marine Park
activities.description: Marine park with dolphin encounters, sea lion shows, stingray touch pools, and educational presentations.

activities.name: Shipwreck Island Waterpark
activities.description: Family waterpark with slides, wave pool, lazy river, and a 500,000-gallon wave pool.

activities.name: St. Andrews State Park
activities.description: 1,260-acre park on the east end of the beach with excellent snorkeling, fishing, nature trails, and the jetties.

activities.name: Deep-Sea Fishing
activities.description: Charter fishing from Captain Anderson's Marina targeting red snapper, grouper, and king mackerel.

activities.name: ZooWorld Zoological Conservatory
activities.description: Interactive zoo with giraffe feeding, kangaroo walkabout, and exotic animal encounters.

activities.name: Pontoon Boat Rentals
activities.description: Rent a pontoon boat to explore Shell Island, St. Andrews Bay, and the emerald Gulf waters at your own pace.

title: Things to Do in Delray Beach
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Delray Beach FL
pageSubtitle: Atlantic Avenue culture, pristine beaches, and vibrant nightlife in South Florida.
introContent: Delray Beach has been called the Most Fun Small Town in America, and for good reason. Atlantic Avenue is one of South Florida's best strips for dining, shopping, and nightlife, while the beach is pristine and the arts scene continues to grow.

activities.name: Atlantic Avenue
activities.description: Delray's vibrant main street running from the Intracoastal to the beach with over 100 restaurants, shops, and galleries.

activities.name: Delray Beach
activities.description: Beautiful public beach steps from Atlantic Avenue with lifeguards, volleyball courts, and a family-friendly atmosphere.

activities.name: Morikami Museum & Japanese Gardens
activities.description: Museum and 16-acre Japanese garden featuring bonsai collection, tea ceremonies, and rotating art exhibitions.

activities.name: Pineapple Grove Arts District
activities.description: Downtown arts district with galleries, murals, public art, and the monthly Art Walk event.

activities.name: Arts Garage
activities.description: Intimate live music and theater venue on NE 1st Street featuring jazz, blues, rock, and comedy performances.

activities.name: Wakodahatchee Wetlands
activities.description: 50-acre constructed wetlands with a 3/4-mile boardwalk offering incredible birding and wildlife photography.

activities.name: Delray Beach GreenMarket
activities.description: Saturday morning market with local produce, baked goods, artisan crafts, and prepared foods at Old School Square.

activities.name: Old School Square
activities.description: Restored 1913 school campus now serving as a cultural center with galleries, events, and the Fieldhouse.

activities.name: El Rio Trail
activities.description: Paved multi-use trail connecting Boca Raton to Delray Beach through wetlands and neighborhoods.

activities.name: Delray Dining Scene
activities.description: Over 200 restaurants in the downtown area with cuisine from sushi to Italian to farm-to-table American.

title: Things to Do in Boca Raton
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Boca Raton FL
pageSubtitle: Upscale shopping, nature trails, and beautiful beaches in South Palm Beach County.
introContent: Boca Raton is an upscale South Florida city known for the iconic Boca Raton Resort & Club, beautiful beaches, and the sprawling Mizner Park lifestyle center. Nature preserves and excellent dining round out this polished coastal community.

activities.name: Red Reef Park
activities.description: Oceanfront park with a natural reef for snorkeling, a beach, nature trails, and the Gumbo Limbo Nature Center.

activities.name: Gumbo Limbo Nature Center
activities.description: Nature center with sea turtle rehabilitation, aquariums, butterfly garden, and boardwalk trails through mangroves.

activities.name: Mizner Park
activities.description: Upscale open-air shopping, dining, and entertainment complex with a cultural arts center and amphitheater.

activities.name: Boca Raton Resort & Club
activities.description: Legendary 1926 Addison Mizner-designed resort on the Intracoastal with a private beach, golf, and multiple restaurants.

activities.name: Daggerwing Nature Center
activities.description: Nature center in a 40-acre wetland preserve with boardwalk trails, live animal exhibits, and butterfly garden.

activities.name: Boca Raton Museum of Art
activities.description: Contemporary art museum in Mizner Park with rotating exhibitions, outdoor sculpture garden, and art education programs.

activities.name: South Beach Park
activities.description: Boca's main public beach with lifeguards, picnic areas, and a pavilion right on AIA.

activities.name: Town Center at Boca Raton
activities.description: Premier indoor shopping mall with luxury brands including Neiman Marcus, Nordstrom, and Cartier.

activities.name: Osprey Point Golf Course
activities.description: Public golf course at the Boca Raton Airport with a challenging layout and affordable green fees.

activities.name: Spanish River Park
activities.description: 95-acre oceanfront park with beach access, nature trails, and picnic pavilions along the Intracoastal.

title: Things to Do in Hollywood FL
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do in Hollywood FL
pageSubtitle: Broadwalk charm, beach culture, and arts between Fort Lauderdale and Miami.
introContent: Hollywood, Florida sits between Fort Lauderdale and Miami, offering a 2.5-mile oceanfront Broadwalk, vibrant arts scene, and diverse dining. The city combines a laid-back beach vibe with easy access to South Florida's major attractions.

activities.name: Hollywood Beach Broadwalk
activities.description: 2.5-mile oceanfront promenade with restaurants, shops, street performers, and bike rentals along the Atlantic.

activities.name: Hollywood Beach
activities.description: Wide, sandy Atlantic beach with calm waters, lifeguards, and a family-friendly atmosphere.

activities.name: Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino
activities.description: Guitar-shaped luxury resort with gaming, restaurants, concerts, and the massive guitar pool.

activities.name: Downtown Hollywood Arts District
activities.description: Walkable downtown with galleries, murals, live music, and the monthly ArtWalk event on Harrison Street.

activities.name: Anne Kolb Nature Center
activities.description: 1,500-acre urban nature preserve with a 4-story observation tower, mangrove trails, and kayak rentals.

activities.name: Young Circle Arts Park
activities.description: 10-acre public art park with outdoor sculpture, events, and the ArtsPark Gallery.

activities.name: Yellow Green Farmers Market
activities.description: Massive weekend farmers market with over 400 vendors selling food, produce, crafts, and specialty items.

activities.name: Hollywood Arts & Culture Center
activities.description: Community arts center with gallery exhibitions, theater performances, and visual arts classes.

activities.name: Hollywood Bike Rentals
activities.description: Rent bikes or e-bikes to cruise the Broadwalk, connecting paths, and the Hollywood Beach trail system.

activities.name: Diplomat Beach Resort Dining
activities.description: Oceanfront resort with multiple restaurants and bars offering upscale beachfront dining.

title: Things to Do in St. Augustine
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in St. Augustine FL
pageSubtitle: America's oldest city offers 450 years of history, beaches, and cultural treasures.
introContent: St. Augustine is the oldest continuously occupied European settlement in the US, founded by the Spanish in 1565. The charming city features a beautifully preserved historic district, the iconic Castillo de San Marcos, beautiful beaches, and a thriving food scene.

activities.name: Castillo de San Marcos
activities.description: The oldest masonry fort in the continental US, built by the Spanish from 1672-1695 with coquina shell limestone walls.

activities.name: St. George Street
activities.description: Pedestrian-only historic street lined with shops, restaurants, museums, and colonial-era buildings.

activities.name: St. Augustine Alligator Farm
activities.description: Zoo and crocodilian park since 1893 with every species of crocodilian in the world, plus a zip line and wildlife shows.

activities.name: St. Augustine Lighthouse & Maritime Museum
activities.description: Climb 219 steps to the top of this 1874 lighthouse for panoramic views, plus explore the museum and dark of the moon ghost tours.

activities.name: St. Augustine Beach
activities.description: Beautiful Atlantic beach with the St. Johns County Ocean Pier, surf breaks, and a relaxed beach town atmosphere.

activities.name: Flagler College
activities.description: Stunning former Ponce de Leon Hotel built by Henry Flagler in 1888 with Spanish Renaissance architecture and Tiffany stained glass.

activities.name: Pirate & Treasure Museum
