activities.description: World-class collection of authentic pirate artifacts including one of only two Jolly Roger flags known to exist.

activities.name: Old Town Trolley Tours
activities.description: Narrated hop-on, hop-off trolley tour visiting 23 stops throughout the historic district and waterfront.

activities.name: Fort Matanzas National Monument
activities.description: Spanish watchtower built in 1742 accessible by free ferry boat, with pristine beach and nature trails.

activities.name: San Sebastian Winery
activities.description: Downtown winery offering free tours and tastings, plus a rooftop bar with views of the historic city.

title: Things to Do in Jacksonville
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do in Jacksonville FL
pageSubtitle: Sprawling river city with beaches, breweries, and outdoor adventures.
introContent: Jacksonville is the largest city by area in the contiguous US, spanning the St. Johns River to the Atlantic coast. The city offers diverse neighborhoods, a growing craft beer scene, beautiful beaches, and 80,000 acres of parks.

activities.name: Jacksonville Beach
activities.description: Atlantic beach community with a fishing pier, boardwalk, surf breaks, and a lively strip of restaurants and bars.

activities.name: Cummer Museum of Art & Gardens
activities.description: Art museum on the St. Johns River with over 5,000 works, formal Olmsted gardens, and free admission on Tuesdays.

activities.name: Jacksonville Zoo and Gardens
activities.description: Award-winning zoo with a walkthrough Africa exhibit, jaguar habitat, botanical gardens, and the Range of the Jaguar.

activities.name: San Marco District
activities.description: Historic neighborhood with tree-lined streets, boutique shopping, restaurants, and the San Marco Theatre.

activities.name: Big Talbot Island State Park
activities.description: Coastal park famous for its Boneyard Beach, where weathered oak and cedar trees create a stunning landscape.

activities.name: EverBank Stadium
activities.description: Home of the Jacksonville Jaguars with the world's largest video scoreboards and a riverfront location.

activities.name: Jacksonville Craft Beer Scene
activities.description: Thriving brewery scene with Bold City, Intuition Ale Works, Aardwolf Brewing, and Wicked Barley.

activities.name: Riverside & Five Points
activities.description: Hip neighborhood with independent shops, restaurants, street art, and the weekly Riverside Arts Market.

activities.name: Fort Clinch State Park
activities.description: Well-preserved Civil War-era fort on Amelia Island with beaches, fishing, hiking, and living history reenactments.

activities.name: Florida Theatre
activities.description: Stunning 1927 Mediterranean Revival movie palace hosting concerts, comedy, and performing arts in downtown.

title: Things to Do in Amelia Island
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do on Amelia Island FL
pageSubtitle: Victorian charm, pristine beaches, and resort luxury on Florida's northernmost barrier island.
introContent: Amelia Island is a 13-mile barrier island off northeast Florida known for its Victorian-era downtown Fernandina Beach, luxury resorts, and pristine natural beaches. It is the only US location to have been under eight different flags.

activities.name: Historic Fernandina Beach
activities.description: 50-block Victorian seaport village with galleries, restaurants, boutiques, and Centre Street's charming main strip.

activities.name: Amelia Island Beaches
activities.description: 13 miles of uncrowded, natural Atlantic beach with horseback riding, surf fishing, and sea turtle nesting.

activities.name: Fort Clinch State Park
activities.description: Well-preserved 1847 brick fort with living history programs, pristine beaches, nature trails, and fishing.

activities.name: Omni Amelia Island Resort
activities.description: Luxury beachfront resort with 36 holes of championship golf, a full-service spa, and multiple restaurants.

activities.name: Isle of Eight Flags Shrimp Festival
activities.description: Annual spring festival celebrating Amelia Island's shrimping heritage with food, art, music, and a pirate parade.

activities.name: Horseback Beach Rides
activities.description: Guided horseback riding along the Atlantic beach - one of few Florida beaches where horses are permitted.

activities.name: Kayaking & Paddleboarding
activities.description: Explore the marshes, tidal creeks, and Intracoastal Waterway by kayak or SUP with guided eco-tours.

activities.name: Amelia Island Dining Scene
activities.description: From upscale dining at the Ritz-Carlton to casual seafood at The Salty Pelican, the island offers diverse culinary options.

activities.name: Amelia Island State Park
activities.description: 200-acre park on the south end of the island with fishing, hiking, birdwatching, and George Crady Bridge Fishing Pier.

activities.name: Amelia Island Museum of History
activities.description: Museum in the former county jail telling the story of 4,000 years of island history, including the Eight Flags era.

title: Things to Do in Tallahassee
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do in Tallahassee FL
pageSubtitle: Florida's capital city with canopy roads, college football, and natural springs.
introContent: Tallahassee is Florida's capital city and home to Florida State University and Florida A&M University. The city is known for its canopy roads, natural springs, college football culture, and a thriving local food and music scene.

activities.name: Florida State Capitol
activities.description: Florida's seat of government with a 22nd-floor observation deck offering panoramic views and free admission.

activities.name: FSU Seminoles Football
activities.description: Doak Campbell Stadium is one of the most iconic college football venues, home to the Florida State Seminoles.

activities.name: Wakulla Springs State Park
activities.description: One of the world's deepest freshwater springs with glass-bottom boat tours, jungle boat rides, and a historic lodge.

activities.name: Museum of Florida History
activities.description: Free museum exploring 12,000 years of Florida history from mastodons to space exploration.

activities.name: Canopy Roads
activities.description: Five historic roads with live oak canopies draped in Spanish moss - scenic drives unique to Tallahassee.

activities.name: Alfred B. Maclay Gardens State Park
activities.description: Ornamental gardens and lake swimming in a 1,176-acre park with hiking, biking, and kayaking.

activities.name: Railroad Square Art District
activities.description: Creative district in a former industrial area with galleries, studios, shops, and First Friday events.

activities.name: Midtown & Market District
activities.description: Walkable neighborhoods with local restaurants, craft breweries, and boutique shopping.

activities.name: St. Marks River Paddling
activities.description: Paddle the spring-fed St. Marks River through forests and limestone bluffs to the Gulf of Mexico.

activities.name: Proof Brewing Company
activities.description: Tallahassee's largest craft brewery with a massive taproom, outdoor beer garden, food trucks, and live music.

title: Things to Do in Ormond Beach
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do in Ormond Beach FL
pageSubtitle: The Birthplace of Speed offers quiet beaches, nature, and history north of Daytona.
introContent: Ormond Beach is known as the Birthplace of Speed, where early automobile racing pioneers first tested their cars on the hard-packed sand. Today it offers a quieter alternative to Daytona with beautiful beaches, state parks, and the historic Casements.

activities.name: Ormond Beach
activities.description: Quiet, family-friendly Atlantic beach north of Daytona with car-free stretches and scenic dune walks.

activities.name: The Casements
activities.description: John D. Rockefeller's former winter home on the Halifax River, now a cultural center with tours, gardens, and events.

activities.name: Tomoka State Park
activities.description: Nature park where the Tomoka and Halifax Rivers meet with kayaking, fishing, hiking, and a Fred Dana Marsh museum.

activities.name: Bulow Plantation Ruins Historic State Park
activities.description: Ruins of an 1821 sugar plantation with interpretive trails through the remains of the sugar mill and slave quarters.

activities.name: Ormond Scenic Loop & Trail
activities.description: 30-mile scenic drive and bike path through canopy roads, past Tomoka State Park, and along the Halifax River.

activities.name: Halifax River Fishing
activities.description: Intracoastal fishing for redfish, trout, and flounder from parks, bridges, and guided kayak fishing tours.

activities.name: Riviera Country Club
activities.description: Historic 18-hole golf course along the Halifax River in a scenic Ormond Beach setting.

activities.name: Ormond Beach Farmers Market
activities.description: Thursday morning market at Rockefeller Gardens with local produce, baked goods, and crafts.

activities.name: Birthplace of Speed Park
activities.description: Waterfront park commemorating the early automobile racing history that started on Ormond's beach in 1903.

activities.name: Andy Romano Beachfront Park
activities.description: Oceanfront park with a splash pad, playground, observation tower, and beautiful sunrise views.

title: Things to Do in Port Canaveral
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do at Port Canaveral FL
pageSubtitle: Cruise port dining, rocket launches, fishing, and Space Coast attractions.
introContent: Port Canaveral is one of the world's busiest cruise ports and a gateway to Kennedy Space Center. The Cove at Port Canaveral offers waterfront dining, the Exploration Tower, fishing charters, and some of the best rocket launch viewing in Florida.

activities.name: Cruise Ship Departures
activities.description: Major cruise lines including Disney, Royal Caribbean, Carnival, and Norwegian depart from Port Canaveral's modern terminals.

activities.name: Exploration Tower
activities.description: Seven-story interactive attraction with exhibits, observation deck, and panoramic views of launches and cruise ships.

activities.name: The Cove at Port Canaveral
activities.description: Waterfront dining and entertainment district with Grills, Fishlips, Rusty's, and other seafood restaurants.

activities.name: Fishing Charters
activities.description: Deep-sea and inshore fishing trips departing from the port targeting mahi-mahi, snapper, grouper, and more.

activities.name: Rocket Launch Viewing
activities.description: Watch SpaceX Falcon 9 and NASA rockets launch from nearby pads visible from Jetty Park and the port area.

activities.name: Jetty Park
activities.description: 4.5-acre beach park at the port entrance perfect for watching cruise ships, fishing, camping, and swimming.

activities.name: Dolphin & Nature Tours
activities.description: Eco-tour boats and kayak tours departing from the port to explore the Banana River and spot dolphins and manatees.

activities.name: Cove Shopping
activities.description: Shops and boutiques at the Cove including surf shops, souvenirs, and nautical gear.

activities.name: Kennedy Space Center
activities.description: Just 15 minutes from the port, KSC offers full-day space exploration experiences with shuttle exhibits and astronaut encounters.

activities.name: Canaveral Lock Park
activities.description: Public park at the Banana River locks with fishing, manatee viewing, and a close-up view of the Intracoastal Waterway.

title: Things to Do Near Disney World
pageHeading: 30 Best Things to Do Near Walt Disney World
pageSubtitle: Beyond the parks - attractions, dining, and activities near Walt Disney World in Orlando.
introContent: Walt Disney World covers 25,000 acres in Central Florida, but the surrounding area offers plenty of entertainment beyond the parks. From shopping at Disney Springs to airboat rides, there is no shortage of activities near the happiest place on Earth.

activities.name: Disney Springs
activities.description: Over 100 shops and restaurants at Disney's open-air entertainment complex with no park admission required.

activities.name: Universal Studios Florida
activities.description: Just 20 minutes from Disney, Universal offers the Wizarding World of Harry Potter, Velocicoaster, and movie-themed rides.

activities.name: Wild Florida Airboats & Safari
activities.description: 30 minutes from Disney with airboat tours through wetlands and a drive-through safari park.

activities.name: Disney Golf Courses
activities.description: Four championship courses at Walt Disney World open to the public, including the Palmer-designed course.

activities.name: Splitsville Luxury Lanes
activities.description: Upscale bowling and dining at Disney Springs with a rooftop patio and hand-rolled sushi.

activities.name: Old Town Kissimmee
activities.description: Retro entertainment district 15 minutes from Disney with shops, rides, and Saturday night classic car cruises.

activities.name: Disney's Typhoon Lagoon
activities.description: Tropical-themed water park with one of the world's largest wave pools and water slides.

activities.name: Cocoa Beach Day Trip
activities.description: The closest Atlantic beach to Disney, about an hour east, with surfing, the pier, and Kennedy Space Center nearby.

title: Things to Do Near Universal Studios
pageHeading: 25 Best Things to Do Near Universal Orlando
pageSubtitle: Dining, entertainment, and attractions surrounding Universal Studios Florida.
introContent: Universal Orlando Resort sits on International Drive in the heart of Orlando's tourist corridor. The surrounding area offers International Drive attractions, CityWalk entertainment, and easy access to Orlando's dining and shopping scene.

activities.name: Universal CityWalk
activities.description: Free-admission entertainment complex at Universal with restaurants, clubs, mini-golf, and a movie theater.

activities.name: ICON Park
activities.description: Entertainment complex on I-Drive with The Wheel observation ride, SEA LIFE Aquarium, and Madame Tussauds.

activities.name: Restaurant Row
activities.description: Sand Lake Road restaurant corridor with dozens of upscale and casual dining options minutes from Universal.

activities.name: Orlando International Premium Outlets
activities.description: Large outlet mall on International Drive with over 150 brand-name stores.

activities.name: Topgolf Orlando
activities.description: High-tech driving range and entertainment venue with food, drinks, and climate-controlled hitting bays.

activities.name: Cafe Tu Tu Tango
activities.description: Artist-themed tapas restaurant where live artists paint while you dine - a unique I-Drive experience.

activities.name: Gatorland
activities.description: The Alligator Capital of the World is 20 minutes south with zip lines, shows, and thousands of gators.

activities.name: I-Drive NASCAR
activities.description: Indoor go-kart racing on International Drive with multi-level tracks and racing simulators.

title: Things to Do Near SeaWorld Orlando
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do Near SeaWorld Orlando
pageSubtitle: Attractions, dining, and activities surrounding SeaWorld on International Drive.
introContent: SeaWorld Orlando sits in the International Drive corridor near some of Orlando's best dining and entertainment. Aquatica water park is right next door, and I-Drive attractions are minutes away.

activities.name: Aquatica Orlando
activities.description: SeaWorld's water park with high-speed water slides, lazy rivers, a wave pool, and a beach area.

activities.name: ICON Park
activities.description: The Wheel, SEA LIFE Aquarium, and Madame Tussauds just up International Drive from SeaWorld.

activities.name: Restaurant Row
activities.description: Sand Lake Road's restaurant corridor features dozens of dining options from fine dining to casual eats.

activities.name: Discovery Cove
activities.description: All-inclusive day resort by SeaWorld where guests swim with dolphins, snorkel in a reef, and relax on beaches.

activities.name: Pointe Orlando
activities.description: Shopping and dining complex on I-Drive with restaurants, bars, and entertainment venues.

activities.name: Fun Spot America
activities.description: Family amusement park on I-Drive with go-karts, roller coasters, and the SkyCoaster - free admission.

activities.name: SEA LIFE Orlando Aquarium
activities.description: Walk-through aquarium at ICON Park with over 5,000 sea creatures and a 360-degree ocean tunnel.

activities.name: Topgolf Orlando
activities.description: High-tech driving range minutes from SeaWorld with food, drinks, and entertainment.

title: Things to Do Near Kennedy Space Center
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do Near Kennedy Space Center
pageSubtitle: Space Coast attractions, beaches, and nature surrounding NASA's launch headquarters.
introContent: Kennedy Space Center sits on Merritt Island between the Indian River and the Atlantic Ocean. The surrounding Space Coast offers pristine beaches, world-class wildlife viewing, and waterfront dining within minutes of the visitor complex.

activities.name: Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge
activities.description: 140,000-acre refuge sharing land with KSC, with the Black Point Wildlife Drive, hiking trails, and diverse bird species.

activities.name: Playalinda Beach
activities.description: Pristine, undeveloped beach in Canaveral National Seashore - one of the closest beaches to the launch pads.

activities.name: Cocoa Beach
activities.description: 15 minutes south of KSC with surfing, the Cocoa Beach Pier, Ron Jon Surf Shop, and waterfront dining.

activities.name: Port Canaveral
activities.description: Major cruise port with waterfront restaurants, fishing charters, and the Exploration Tower observation deck.

activities.name: Playalinda Brewing Company
activities.description: Space-themed craft brewery in downtown Titusville with creative beers, wood-fired pizza, and a lively taproom.

activities.name: Space View Park Titusville
activities.description: The best public rocket launch viewing spot with unobstructed views across the Indian River to the launch pads.

activities.name: Dolphin & Manatee Kayak Tours
activities.description: Guided kayak tours through the Indian River Lagoon and Banana River to see dolphins and manatees up close.

activities.name: American Space Museum
activities.description: Small but informative museum in downtown Titusville dedicated to the history of American space exploration.

title: Things to Do Near Port Canaveral
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do Near Port Canaveral
pageSubtitle: Pre-cruise and post-cruise activities, dining, and attractions near Port Canaveral.
introContent: Port Canaveral is a world-class cruise port welcoming millions of passengers annually. Whether you are arriving early or extending your trip, the surrounding area offers beaches, space attractions, and waterfront dining minutes from the terminals.

activities.name: Kennedy Space Center
activities.description: 15 minutes from the port with full-day space exploration experiences - perfect for the day before or after a cruise.

activities.name: Cocoa Beach
activities.description: The closest beach to the port with surfing, dining, and the famous Ron Jon Surf Shop.

activities.name: The Cove Waterfront Dining
activities.description: Grills, Fishlips, Rusty's, and more - all overlooking the port with views of cruise ships and fishing boats.

activities.name: Jetty Park
activities.description: Beach and campground at the port entrance where you can watch cruise ships pass within yards.

activities.name: Exploration Tower
activities.description: Seven-story observation tower with interactive exhibits and views of launches, cruise ships, and the coast.

activities.name: Deep-Sea Fishing
activities.description: Charter fishing trips departing from the port targeting offshore gamefish in the Gulf Stream.

activities.name: Merritt Island Wildlife Refuge
activities.description: Scenic wildlife drive and nature trails minutes from the port with alligators, birds, and manatees.

activities.name: Ron Jon Surf Shop
activities.description: The world's largest surf shop in Cocoa Beach, open 24 hours, with everything from surfboards to souvenirs.

title: Things to Do Near Daytona International Speedway
pageHeading: 15 Best Things to Do Near Daytona International Speedway
pageSubtitle: Racing, beaches, and entertainment surrounding the World Center of Racing.
introContent: Daytona International Speedway is the home of the Daytona 500 and sits in the heart of Daytona Beach. Whether you are in town for a race or just visiting, the surrounding area offers beaches, boardwalk fun, and motorsports attractions.

activities.name: Speedway Tours & Motorsports Hall of Fame
activities.description: Behind-the-scenes tours of the 2.5-mile superspeedway, Victory Lane, and the Motorsports Hall of Fame of America.

activities.name: Daytona Beach
activities.description: 23 miles of beach just 15 minutes from the Speedway with the classic boardwalk and driving-on-the-beach tradition.

activities.name: Tanger Outlets Daytona Beach
activities.description: 80+ outlet stores adjacent to the Speedway with brand-name shopping.

activities.name: Daytona Beach Boardwalk
activities.description: Classic oceanfront boardwalk with rides, arcades, and the Main Street Pier - 15 minutes from the track.

activities.name: Richard Petty Driving Experience
activities.description: Drive a real NASCAR stock car on the Daytona banking or ride along with a pro driver.

activities.name: Daytona Beach Craft Breweries
activities.description: Local breweries including Tomoka Brewing, Ormond Brewing, and Beachside Brewing offer craft beer near the track.

activities.name: Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse
activities.description: Florida's tallest lighthouse at 175 feet, 20 minutes south of the Speedway with panoramic views.

activities.name: Downtown Daytona Beach
activities.description: Revitalized downtown with restaurants, bars, and entertainment along Beach Street and ISB Boulevard.

title: Things to Do Near Clearwater Beach
pageHeading: 20 Best Things to Do Near Clearwater Beach
pageSubtitle: Beyond the sand - attractions, dining, and island-hopping near America's best beach.

activities.name: Jaxson's Ice Cream Parlor

title: Kid-Friendly Things to Do in Miami
pageHeading: 10 Best Kid-Friendly Activities in Miami FL
pageSubtitle: Family beaches, zoo, aquarium, and science museum in the Magic City.
introContent: Miami offers a world of family-friendly activities from Zoo Miami and the Frost Science Museum to calm Key Biscayne beaches and Everglades airboat rides. Kids are entertained and educated at every turn.

activities.name: Zoo Miami
activities.description: The largest and oldest zoological garden in Florida with over 3,000 animals and a splash play area.

activities.name: Frost Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive science museum with a living aquarium, planetarium, and rooftop exhibits.

activities.name: Crandon Park Beach
activities.description: Calm, shallow waters on Key Biscayne - one of the safest beaches for young children.

activities.name: Everglades Airboat Ride
activities.description: Kids love the thrill of airboat rides spotting alligators in the River of Grass.

activities.name: Miami Seaquarium
activities.description: Dolphin shows, sea lion encounters, and marine exhibits on Virginia Key.

activities.name: Jungle Island
activities.description: Interactive animal park in Watson Island with sloth, flamingo, and lemur encounters.

activities.name: Miami Children's Museum
activities.description: Interactive museum on Watson Island with hands-on exhibits designed for kids.

activities.name: Venetian Pool
activities.description: Historic public swimming pool in Coral Gables carved from coral rock with waterfalls and grottos.
